svg.title: Pixel Eruv — Backend services (Go microservices and communication)
svg.desc: The three Go services (Pusher, World Simulator, LiveKit Bridge), extensions, their internal modules, and every communication channel between them and the surrounding state stores and external services.
svg.text: Pusher (network proxy) · World Simulator (spatial authority + replication gateway) · LiveKit Bridge (media sync)
register · spawn · update · triggers · zones · KV

g.text: thin WebSocket proxy · no game logic
spatial authority · ECS · triggers · replication · AOI
NPCs · triggers · zones · LLM agents · objects — via NATS only
• All backend services communicate via NATS — no direct service-to-service RPC.
• The World Sim is the spatial authority: ECS, spatial index, triggers, zone boundaries, replication.
• Extensions own all gameplay behavior (NPCs, triggers, zones, AI) — the kernel only moves player avatars.
• Redis is isolated to LiveKit — no other service may use it.
• NATS Core is at-most-once (ephemeral); JetStream KV is semi-persistent with kv.Watch.
• Replication: World Sim → NATS Core → Pusher → Browser (never direct to client).
